# ghost-giscus-plugin

> A tiny, dependency-free helper that places giscus comments at a spot you choose. It does not wrap or replace giscus: it only prepares the mount point (an element with the `giscus` class) at the position you configure, so the official giscus script finds it and mounts there. Works through Ghost code injection with no theme editing. MIT-licensed, by GreedyLabs.

## Install

Add two scripts to Ghost's Settings -> Code injection -> Site Footer. This plugin MUST come before the official giscus tag, because giscus decides where to mount the instant it runs:

    <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/GreedyLabs/ghost-giscus-plugin@1/giscus-mount.min.js"
            data-target=".gh-content"
            data-place="after"
            data-class="gh-comments gh-canvas"
            data-padding-bottom="48"></script>

    <script src="https://giscus.app/client.js"
            data-repo="[YOUR REPO]"
            data-repo-id="[REPO ID]"
            data-category="[CATEGORY]"
            data-category-id="[CATEGORY ID]"
            data-mapping="pathname"
            data-theme="preferred_color_scheme"
            crossorigin="anonymous"
            async></script>

Get the giscus values (repo, repo-id, category, category-id) from https://giscus.app.

## Options

All behavior is configured with `data-*` attributes on the plugin script tag:

- `data-target` — CSS selector for the anchor element (e.g. `.gh-comments`)
- `data-place` — `append`, `prepend`, `before`, `after`, or `replace`
- `data-class` — extra class(es) on the mount wrapper (inherit theme styling)
- `data-padding-top` / `data-padding-right` / `data-padding-bottom` / `data-padding-left` — wrapper padding in px

The target selector's absence is the page guard: when it matches nothing (and no `.giscus` was placed manually), the plugin removes the sibling giscus script so comments do not fall back to the page footer. Pass `data-guard="false"` to opt out. `replace` clears the target and turns it into the giscus mount, which reproduces a theme's `<div class="gh-comments gh-canvas giscus"></div>` from code injection alone.

## Links

- Live demo and configurator: https://ghost-giscus-plugin.greedylabs.kr/en/
- Source and issues: https://github.com/GreedyLabs/ghost-giscus-plugin
